why_interesting: PGGT1B has the largest stimulated footprint in the campaign, 3014 DE genes at Stim8hr, with K562 DE 1.
main_risk: proposal-only until an orthogonal perturbation reproduces the stimulated CD4+ footprint.
what_would_weaken: Loss of the stimulated DE footprint after orthogonal knockdown would lower priority.
rationale: hypothesis to test: PGGT1B has a stimulated CD4+ footprint, little non-immune transfer, and 0 CollecTRI targets
assay: repeat CRISPRi or orthogonal knockdown in stimulated primary CD4+ T cells; measure activation markers and targeted RNA-seq at 8h and 48h
